titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Barker Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 11, 2018 at 03:03

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of poetry and prose by George Barker in autograph, typescript, and printed forms, including poems with autograph revisions by T. S. Eliot. Also present are a small group of letters to Barker from editors and colleagues including Brian Higgins and Oscar Williams, six line drawings by Barker, and a collection of ten typescripts of poems by William Bell.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from C. A. Stonehill on the E. J. and F. W. Beinecke Rare Book Endowment Fund, 1967.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The George Barker Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George Barker Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
The contents of this finding aid are based on the collection inventory provided by the dealer.
This collection includes materials previously identified by the following call number: Uncat MS Vault 55.

bioghist.head: George Barker (1913-1991)
bioghist.p: George Granville Barker (1913-1991), English poet and author.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of poetry and prose by George Barker in manuscript, typescript, and printed forms, including poems with autograph revisions by T. S. Eliot. Also present are a small group of letters to Barker from editors and colleagues including Brian Higgins and Oscar Williams, six line drawings by Barker, and a collection of ten poems by William Bell.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Writings, 1933-1967. II. Correspondence, 1934-1965. Series III. Other Papers, undated.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into four subseries: Poetry, Prose, Proof Sheets, and Printed Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript pages of poems used in or gathered for the book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph and typescript drafts of poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript, revised, of a twenty-four line poem, accompanied by a typed letter, signed, from Derek Verschoyle accepting the poem for publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph draft beginning "Ichor-pressing my hands whence flow / Liquids of the spirit's fruit" with pencilled note by Barker: "Poem written about 1937."

did.unittitle: Poems published in News of the World

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts (11 p.) of three poems: "To My Son," "News of the World," and "Turn on Your Side and Bear the Day to Me," with revisions by T. S. Eliot.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mimeographed typescript (63 p.) for internal use at British Broadcasting Company, with Barker's pencilled autograph revisions; also an additional two pages of brief autograph notes concerning the play.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two octavo notebooks containing 235 pages of Barker's autograph drafts of lyrics; also present are two pencil drawings and 7 pages of typescript drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Corrected typescript with autograph revisions, variant autograph drafts of 37 lyrics, and a typed title page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph draft (7 p.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript (70 p.) in a notebook; also present are notes for lectures, teaching schedules, and "Notes on Michelangelo."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph and typescript versions of the poems "Lake Erie," "Pan Am Flight 462 Jan. 14 1966," "Inscription for the Gazebo at York University," and "Dupe Eatherly."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript (7 p.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon typescript of a translation into French by David Gascoyne of an essay by Barker; signed by Barker and Gascoyne, with a brief explanatory typescript note by Barker. The original essay was titled "Therefore All Poems are Elegies."

Accompanied by pages 9-12 only of a typescript radio talk (in French) possibly broadcast in the BBC Overseas Service, discussing Barker's "The View from a Blind I." Approximately 1-1/2 pages are devoted to a critical assessment of the book, the remainder to a French translation of the poem "I Walked by a Window in Ireland," followed by the original English text. At the foot of page 12 is a pencilled note by Barker to "David" asking for a translation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript notes dated May 27 and June 1 (1965?), apparently a fragment of a diary, together with another 4 pages of typed notes of an autobiographical nature.

did.unittitle: Answers to a questionnaire on Dante, recorded for a BBC program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript, signed, with autograph revisions; accompanied by a typed note from BBC Drama Department returning the script.

did.unittitle: Introduction to Brian Higgins' The Northern Fiddler

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph versions, one draft in pencil, one in red ink; the latter is the closest to the published text.

did.unittitle: "A Little Panegyric for the 60th Birthday of Wystan Auden"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph draft (5 pages) and corrected typescript, signed and dated "York 1967," of material for the third BBC program.

did.unittitle: "The Memoirs of Lord B: notes for autobiography"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript (31 pages) and corrected typescript (1 page). Together with 5 pages of photocopied sheets of "Speculative Synopsis of the Autobiography of Lord B" inscribed to Seymour Lawrence and signed by Barker, with one original typescript page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Galley proofs with autograph corrections, annotations, and additions, with an autograph letter, signed, from his editor at Wishart & Company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advance proof sheets with autograph revisions.

did.unittitle: George Barker, Martin Bell, Charles Causley , Penguin Modern Poets 3 (Harmondsworth, England: Penguin Books Ltd., 1962)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First edition containing twenty-three poems by Barker; signed by Barker on the title page, inscribed by him to "Rose" on page 11, and with autograph notes on each poem on their pages.

did.unittitle: A View from a Blind (London: Faber and Faber, 1962)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First edition, signed by Barker; contains autograph notes, corrections, dates, and other observances throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First edition; Barker is represented by "Autobiograpical [sic] Footnote" which has an autograph correction and an inscription: "G.B. to L.C.R. 1965."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Barker's "Formal Elegy on the Death of Oscar Williams" with autographs corrections and annotations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five leaves cut from periodicals, each bearing a poem by Barker, and each with autograph explanatory notes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into two subseries: Letters from Barker, Letters to Barker.

did.unittitle: Three letters, autograph and typed letters, signed, to his sister "Otto" (Mrs. John Frye)
Typed letter, signed, to William Butler Yeats
Typed draft letter to Secretary of the Overseas Manpower Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains one folder of line drawings by Barker and one folder of poems by William Bell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six line drawings in graphite and ink on paper and cardboard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescripts of unsigned poems bearing a title page "Ten Anonymous Elegies." They are accompanied by a typed note, signed, by Barker: "These poems, which were sent anonymously to me in 1943, I belive [sic] to be the work of the young poet William Bell, who died in 1948. His one published book Mountains beneath the Horizon, was published by Faber & Faber."
